2.solution: For y defined for ALL real P: 2P¬≤+4P+x+4>0 for all P. Disc=16‚ąí8(x+4)<0‚Üíx>‚ąí2. Base: 11‚ąíx>0‚Üíx<11 and 11‚ąíx‚Č†1‚Üíx‚Č†10. Valid integers: x‚ąą(‚ąí2,11)\{10}={‚ąí1,0,...,9}. x=‚ąí4<‚ąí2 ‚Üí NOT possible. Answer: D.

3.solution: AP condition: 2log(pa)=log(4a)+log(9a)=log(36a¬≤). So (pa)¬≤=36a¬≤ ‚Üí p¬≤=36 ‚Üí p=¬Ī6. For logs to be defined: a>0 (needed for log4a,log9a). With a>0: pa>0 requires p>0‚Üíp=6 only. One value. Answer: 1.

5.solution: Let a=logx, b=logy. 6a+2b=2‚Üí3a+b=1; a+16b=4. From first: b=1‚ąí3a. Sub: a+16‚ąí48a=4‚Üí‚ąí47a=‚ąí12‚Üía=12/47, b=11/47. log(xy)=a+b=23/47. Answer: A.

6.solution: Let p=log a, q=log b. ab=10‚Āī‚Üíp+q=4. p¬≥+q¬≥=(p+q)(p¬≤‚ąípq+q¬≤)=4(p¬≤‚ąípq+q¬≤)=24‚Üíp¬≤‚ąípq+q¬≤=6. The expression equals 6 (divisible by 6>3). Answer: 6.

7.solution: Let u=P^(log_q x). u¬≥‚ąí12u¬≤+47u‚ąí56=4‚Üíu¬≥‚ąí12u¬≤+47u‚ąí60=0‚Üí(u‚ąí3)(u‚ąí4)(u‚ąí5)=0‚Üíu‚ąą{3,4,5}. For x=q: log_q x=1‚ÜíP^1=P=u. Need P‚ąą{3,4,5}, valid since P>1. So x=q is possible. Answer: C (q).

9.solution: ‚ąö2+‚ąö72=‚ąö2+6‚ąö2=7‚ąö2. Note 98=2√ó7¬≤, so ‚ąö98=7‚ąö2, i.e., 98^(1/2)=7‚ąö2‚Üílog‚āČ‚āą(7‚ąö2)=1/2. Equation: log_x(1/2)=‚ąí1/3‚Üíx^(‚ąí1/3)=1/2‚Üíx^(1/3)=2‚Üíx=8. Answer: D.
